page: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core.

This server includes 5669 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. The Intel Xeon processor provides advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate cryptographic, vector, and deep learning operations.

In benchmark evaluations, the D96ds_v6 demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, achieving high marks in Passmark CPU and Geekbench multi-core tests. It excels in static web serving throughput and image processing, placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-threaded operations, such as bzip3 decompression and stress-ng multi-core tests, fall within the top 25%, supported by a strong uncached memory read bandwidth of 257.11 GB/sec.

Given its high resource density and balanced CPU-to-memory ratio, the server offers qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale enterprise applications that require local high-speed storage without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware. It is highly suited for database operations, web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure D96ds_v6 is a high-capacity general-purpose virtual machine from the Ddsv6 family, powered by a 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of RAM and 5669 GB of local storage, operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The architecture supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX for accelerated mathematical and cryptographic computations. In benchmark testing, the server delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, static web serving, and image processing, alongside strong database and multi-threaded decompression capabilities. This configuration provides a balanced resource profile, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for demanding enterprise workloads. It is highly suited for database hosting, large-scale web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
